title: Energy & Plant Engineer (SCEM Certified)
description:
Job Description

  • Drive energy efficiency & sustainability: Lead site energy management initiatives, audits, and improvement projects in line with ISO50001 & regulatory requirements.
  • Monitor energy performance: Track energy/water usage, implement conservation measures, and report savings & carbon reduction.
  • Lead Energy Management Team: Conduct quarterly reviews, collaborate with ESCO consultants, and submit annual reports (EUR & EEIP) to NEA.
  • Maintain utility systems: Oversee utilities availability, performance tracking, preventive maintenance, and coordination with contractors.
  • Plant equipment reliability: Ensure equipment compliance, preventive maintenance strategy, and facility management.
  • Technical support & troubleshooting: Work with maintenance and production teams for root-cause analysis and issue resolution.
  • CapEx & project management: Develop, plan, execute, and control capital projects (budget, schedule, safety & documentation).
  • Equipment specification & upgrades: Design/select equipment that meets technical requirements and improve efficiency.
  • Process safety assurance: Participate in HAZOP, QRA, safety case reviews, and implement safety measures.
  • Change management compliance: Ensure all process changes follow proper Management of Change (MoC) procedures

    REQUIREMENTS
  • Bachelor's degree in mechanical/chemical engineering.
  • Applicant must have Singapore Certified Energy Manager certification to be considered for the position. (Kindly indicate the certification in your CV or attached the certificate as a supporting document during application)
  • 5+ years of experience in a manufacturing industry (Process/Equipment Optimization and Project Management)
  • Experience with continuous improvement processes (e.g., Six Sigma, Lean, 5S, etc.) will be advantageous
  • Experience with ASPEN PIMS and project managementExperience in Digital data collection/analytics tools, databases like OEE , Testify.
  • Ability to apply fundamental engineering and business principles
  • Possess technical knowledge to solve process engineering issues
  • Skills in successfully overcoming challenges and identifying opportunities for improvements
  • Strong teamwork and interpersonal skills
  • Strong organizational and prioritization skills
  • Working knowledge of safety, environmental, and personnel rules and regulations
  • Cross regional communication/networking skills to work with counterparts across different time zones
  • Willing to work in Jurong Island.


We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.
GMP Technologies (S) Pte Ltd | EA Licence: 11C3793 | EA Personnel: Dianna | Registration No: R1327268

This is in partnership with the Employment and Employability Institute Pte Ltd (e2i).

e2i is the empowering network for workers and employers seeking employment and employability solutions. e2i serves as a bridge between workers and employers, connecting with workers to offer job security through job-matching, career guidance and skills upgrading services, and partnering employers to address their manpower needs through recruitment, training, and job redesign solutions. e2i is a tripartite initiative of the National Trades Union Congress set up to support nation-wide manpower and skills upgrading initiatives.

By applying for this role, you consent to GMP Recruitment Services (S) Pte Ltds PDPA and e2is PDPA.
